DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is overruled. Cross-Defendant has ten (10) days to answer. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1320(g).)

The Cross-Complaint states a single cause of action for financial elder abuse under Welfare and Institutions Code section 15610.30. Cross-Defendant's Demurrer is based on Cross-Complainant's failure to plead enhanced remedies for financial elder abuse under Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657.5 with factual particularity. The basis for Cross-Defendant's Demurrer is improper. "[A] demurrer cannot rightfully be sustained to part of a cause of action or to a particular type of damage or remedy." (Kong v. City of Hawaiian Gardens Redevelopment Agency (2002) 108 Cal.App.4th 1547, 1562 ["a demurrer tests the sufficiency of the factual allegations of the complaint rather than the relief suggested in the prayer of the complaint"]; Grieves v. Superior Court (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 159, 163 [punitive damage allegations were not subject to demurrer].) Accordingly, Cross-Defendant's Demurrer is overruled.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT HEBREW HOME FOR THE AGED DISABLED's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT:

Cross-Defendant Hebrew Home for Aged Disabled's Motion to Strike Portions of the First Amended Cross-Complaint is granted in part with leave to amend and denied in part.

First, Cross-Complainant's Prayer for Relief includes a request for punitive damages pursuant to Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657.5, subdivisions (c) and (d). (FACC, 19:24.) Cross-Complainant alleges that Cross-Defendant's employees represented that: (1) Cross-Defendant would submit insurance claims to cover Cross-Complainant's care; (2) insurance would cover the costs of Cross-Complainant's care; (3) Cross-Complainant was entitled to a refund of any amounts paid by Cross-Complainant, which were covered by insurance; and (4) Cross-Defendant would keep Cross-Complainant's Power of Attorney apprised of Cross-Complainant's condition. (FACC, 18, 20-22, 31, 34-37, 39-42, 47, 53-55, 63, 66.) Cross-Complainant refers to Cross-Defendant's employees in the allegations. However, Cross-Complainant fails to allege that Cross-Defendant, as the employer, "had advance knowledge of the unfitness of the employee and employed him or her with a conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others or authorized or ratified the wrongful conduct for which the damages are awarded or was personally guilty of oppression, fraud, or malice." (Civ. Code, sec. 3294(b).) Although punitive damages are recoverable under Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657.5, subdivision (c), "[w]ith respect to a corporate employer, the advance knowledge and conscious disregard, authorization, ratification or act of oppression, fraud, or malice must be on the part of an officer, director, or managing agent of the corporation." (Civ. Code, sec. 3294(b); see Welf. & Inst. Code sec. 15657.5(c) ["The standards set forth in subdivision (b) of Section 3294 of the Civil Code regarding the imposition of punitive damages on an employer based upon the acts of an employee shall be satisfied before any punitive damages may be imposed against an employer found liable for financial abuse as defined in Section 15610.30. This subdivision shall not apply to the recovery of compensatory damages or attorney's fees and costs."].) Accordingly, Cross-Defendant's Motion is granted on this ground with 10 days leave to amend pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 436, subdivision (b).

Second, Cross-Complainant's Prayer for Relief seeks attorney's fees pursuant to Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657.5, subdivision (a). (FACC, 19:25.) Cross-Defendant contends that attorney's fees are not authorized for financial elder abuse. However, Cross-Defendant refers to Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657, which provides remedies for physical abuse, neglect, and abandonment. Under Welfare and Institutions Code section 15657.5, statutory attorney's fees are mandatory "[w]here it is proven by a preponderance of the evidence that a defendant is liable for financial abuse, as defined in Section 15610.30, in addition to compensatory damages and all other remedies otherwise provided by law." (Welf. & Inst. Code, sec. 15657.5(a).) Therefore, Cross-Complainant's request for attorney's fees in the Prayer for Relief is proper. Cross-Defendant's Motion is denied on this ground.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
